description: Documentation dependency registry — foundation-repo distributable skeleton. Hand-editable source of truth for cross-file cascades and entity-parity registries. Read by ${CLAUDE_HOME}/hooks/pre-write-guard.sh and the librarian capabilities (rename-history-sync, wikilink-repair, entity-parity, session-close Step 2c). Mirrored into Logs/librarian-manifest.json -> doc_dependencies on each /librarian full run. Pattern matches drift-allowlist.json + cron-log-architecture-exceptions.json (sister config files at hooks/config/).
generated_by: Plan 81 SP01 T-28a (2026-05-08) — Alternative (b) vault-overlay split skeleton

schema_notes.rename_history: Plan 67 Sub-plan 02 T-3 (2026-04-22). Append-only audit trail of renames that have touched this entry's primary / mirrors[]. Each row: {"from": "<old-path>", "to": "<new-path>", "at": "<ISO8601>", "commit": "<sha>"}. Populated by rename-detect.sh at librarian session-close Step 2b. Forward-only: no historical backfill. Empty array means no known renames since instrumentation landed.
schema_notes.vault_overlay: Per-vault entries (engagement directory cascades, vault-architecture hub-spoke cascades, project-tree directory listings, etc.) live in the paired vault-overlay.json file at the same hooks/config/ directory. Consumers merge entries[] and entities{} additively at runtime: overlay rows by .id replace skeleton rows of the same id; overlay entities keys override skeleton keys of the same name. Schema for the overlay: ${CLAUDE_HOME}/schemas/vault-overlay-schema.json (https://stem.peter.dev/schemas/vault-overlay/v1.json). Per Plan 81 SP01 T-28a ratifying Plan 71 _audit-2026-05-05/04-librarian-architect-personalization.md G4 → SP12 skeleton-only resolution.
schema_notes.path_substitution: {project_namespace} placeholder in entities.memory-file.canonical resolves to the harness-encoded home-path namespace (e.g., -Users-jane for /Users/jane). Consumers compute via ${HOME//\//-} or read from user-manifest.json identity.project_namespace. Foundation-shipped value is unresolved; consumer-side resolution is T-28b scope.

entries.notes: Foundation-infra cascade: vault-schema.json type keys must be reflected in both pre-write-guard.sh SCHEMA_KEY case statement and post-write-verify.sh type_map. Adopters extending vault-schema.json must add the new type to both consumers via R-37 lockstep commit (schema + pre-write-guard SCHEMA_KEY + pre-write-guard R-32 allowlist + post-write-verify type_map + per-vault CLAUDE.md + librarian SKILL.md, all in one commit). Path-inferred exceptions list (default: daily-note) lives here; adopters extend in vault-overlay.json to widen the exception set per their vault conventions.

entities._comment: Entity parity registry (foundation skeleton). Each entity declares a canonical source path template with {name} placeholder, an instance-enumerator (glob/file-list/directory-index), the canonical field to compare, and mirrors with strict/presence-only flags. Consumed by librarian capability entity-parity.sh. Foundation skeleton ships the three generic entity types (skill, plan, memory-file) that mirror across vault and ${CLAUDE_HOME} surfaces; per-vault entity types live in vault-overlay.json.

mirrors.notes: Vault design spec for the skill. Present for most runtime skills; absent for a minority of backend-only skills.
Master catalog row. Description column is paraphrased-by-design (summary, not mirror). V1 checks row presence only; content-match semantics deferred to V2.
Master backlog row. Single-line pointer per sentinel pattern; summary text is paraphrased. Presence-only check.
Plan manifest. Older plans may predate the manifest convention, so mirror is optional; when present, top-level status must match spec frontmatter.
Backlog-progress satellite (sentinel pattern). Only exists for plans with session logs; hence optional.

MEMORY.md index entry. Summary after em-dash is paraphrased for scan-readability. Presence-only check. {project_namespace} placeholder resolves consumer-side from harness-encoded home path or user-manifest.json identity.project_namespace.
